intro: Enneastemon seretii, also known as Seret's boxwood, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Enneastemon seretii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
